mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 4.5 percent over a 5-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising consumer credit balances signal synchronized household balance sheet expansion. This dual demand shock lifts corporate revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline by at least 1.5% in annualized forward returns over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial capacity utilization expansion, rising production output directly boosts corporate profit margins. This supply-side acceleration yields positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings catch up to output growth.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with INDPRO growth rates (p > 0.10) across three consecutive business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 230, inflation expectations embed into long-term bond yields, forcing equity risk premiums higher and depressing valuation multiples, which generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Equity risk premium compression fails to materialize, resulting in positive or neutral 20-day forward returns in over 60% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical trend rate of 2.0 percent signals robust aggregate demand. This macroeconomic momentum translates directly into higher corporate cash flows and drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no directional relationship with GDPC1 growth rates, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate signals structural labor market imbalances. When payroll gains accelerate while unemployment stagnates, it indicates productivity-driven earnings expansion, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain more than 5% of forward return variance, and single-series models outperform the joint specification in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a rapid federal funds rate hiking cycle, rising short-term rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity valuations. This monetary tightening regime generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as discount rates outpace earnings growth.
failure_condition: Equity markets demonstrate resilience or positive forward returns during consecutive rate hikes exceeding 50 basis points per quarter, invalidating the discount rate transmission channel.
